_doc: Maps our agent's remediation tools (rex.loop._remediation_tools / tools_registry.json) onto SREGym cluster-control (kubectl/MCP) command TEMPLATES. {ns}/{kind}/{name} are filled at run time by a TargetResolver (kubectl get) — this file encodes ONLY tool semantics, not per-problem resource bindings. expressible=false means our abstract tool has no faithful GENERIC kubectl mapping (the real fix is a problem-specific NetworkPolicy/Secret/cache endpoint), so the action is reported as out-of-action-space rather than faked.

cordon_node.reason: target is a NODE, not a workload — resolver must resolve to a node name

failover_service.reason: no generic kubectl: failover target/endpoint is problem-specific (DNS/Service selector swap)

modify_network_policy.reason: no generic kubectl: the specific NetworkPolicy / quota-policy to disable is problem-specific

renew_certificate.reason: no generic kubectl: the Secret/cert and CA are problem-specific

clear_cache.reason: no generic kubectl: cache endpoint / flush command is app-specific (Redis/Memcached)
